rates come into effect from May 9\n\nThe federal government has increased petrol and diesel prices by nearly Rs15 per litre each for the next week.\n\nIn a notification issued on Friday, the Petroleum Division said new fuel rates will come into effect from May 9.\n\nThe price of petrol has jumped from Rs399.86 to Rs414.78, while the HSD price increased from Rs399.58 to Rs414.58 per litre.\n\nThis marks the third consecutive increase in petrol and diesel prices after cumulative hikes of Rs33.28 on petrol and Rs46.16 on diesel over the previous two weeks.\n\nThe government has been reviewing petroleum prices every Friday night amid global oil market volatility linked to the Iran-Israel conflict.\n\nOil prices were up more than 1% earlier today after renewed fighting broke out between the US and Iran, threatening a shaky ceasefire and dashing hopes for progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas transit route.\n\nBrent crude futures were up $1.41, or 1.41%, at $101.47 a barrel as of 0123 GMT. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US crude futures rose by $1.12, or 1.18%, to $95.93 a barrel. has increased petrol and diesel prices by nearly Rs15 per litre each for the next week.\n\nIn a notification issued on Friday, the Petroleum Division said new fuel rates will come into effect from May 9.\n\nThe price of petrol has jumped from Rs399.86 to Rs414.78, while the HSD price increased from Rs399.58 to Rs414.58 per litre.\n\nThis marks the third consecutive increase in petrol and diesel prices after cumulative hikes of Rs33.28 on petrol and Rs46.16 on diesel over the previous two weeks.\n\nThe government has been reviewing petroleum prices every Friday night amid global oil market volatility linked to the Iran-Israel conflict.\n\nOil prices were up more than 1% earlier today after renewed fighting broke out between the US and Iran, threatening a shaky ceasefire and dashing hopes for progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas transit route.\n\nBrent crude futures were up $1.41, or 1.41%, at $101.47 a barrel as of 0123 GMT. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US crude futures rose by $1.12, or 1.18%, to $95.93 a barrel. #petrolprice #petrolpump #dieselprices #petrol #diesel #breakingnews #arynews #arynewslive \n\nGovt hikes petrol, diesel prices by Rs15 per litre for next week\n\nPetroleum Division says new fuel rates come into effect from May 9\n\nThe federal government has increased petrol and diesel prices by nearly Rs15 per litre each for the next week.\n\nIn a notification issued on Friday, the Petroleum Division said new fuel rates will come into effect from May 9.\n\nThe price of petrol has jumped from Rs399.86 to Rs414.78, while the HSD price increased from Rs399.58 to Rs414.58 per litre.\n\nThis marks the third consecutive increase in petrol and diesel prices after cumulative hikes of Rs33.28 on petrol and Rs46.16 on diesel over the previous two weeks.\n\nThe government has been reviewing petroleum prices every Friday night amid global oil market volatility linked to the Iran-Israel conflict.\n\nOil prices were up more than 1% earlier today after renewed fighting broke out between the US and Iran, threatening a shaky ceasefire and dashing hopes for progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas transit route.\n\nBrent crude futures were up $1.41, or 1.41%, at $101.47 a barrel as of 0123 GMT. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US crude futures rose by $1.12, or 1.18%, to $95.93 a barrel.
